rule: To distill agentsync outcomes for a project, that project's OWN origin must carry the agentsync coordination branch. distill() reads origin/<agentsync_branch> of CAMBIUM_REPO only â€” it will not reach a board hosted on a different repo, and released claims are never reconstructed from history.
reason: In balatron's first cycle distill() returned 0 agentsync items and reported 'no coordination branch found', because balatron's origin has no agentsync branch â€” the team's live board lives on agentsync-remote/aethermoor, and all of balatron's claims had already been released. Cambium silently falls back to context-keeper only, so you lose every agentsync outcome-memory without any error. Verify the branch exists (git ls-remote --heads origin agentsync) before trusting a distill's agentsync count.
triggering_incident: balatron distill on 2026-07-10: sources.agentsync = 'no coordination branch found'; 80/80 items came from context-keeper, 0 from agentsync.

rule: In PR-mode org promotion, promote items to org ONE AT A TIME â€” merge each cambium promotion PR before opening the next. Do not open multiple org promotion PRs against knowledge.json concurrently.
reason: Each promote(item_id, to_scope='org') branches off origin/main and appends to the same knowledge.json items array. Two concurrently-open promotion PRs edit the same region of that array, so the second cannot merge cleanly until the first lands â€” this is the documented non-transactional PR-mode limitation. Serializing (open PR, merge, then promote the next) avoids the conflict entirely.
triggering_incident: knowledge PRs #1 (con-016) and #2 (con-014) were opened back-to-back on 2026-07-10; PR #2 required manual conflict resolution against knowledge.json before it could merge.
